No Poverty.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus supports a connection to eradicating poverty or related targets.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly aims to 'promote healthy physical fitness' and 'develop a habit of regular exercise,' directly supporting Target 3.4 on preventing non-communicable diseases and promoting well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3) and fosters skills like 'teamwork and mutual cooperation,' aligning with Target 4.7 for sustainable development skills.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to employment, economic growth, or labor rights.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to infrastructure development, industrialization, or innovation.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The 'male and female' (男女合班) class format promotes social inclusion irrespective of sex, directly supporting Target 10.2.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course implicitly promotes the use of recreational public spaces (soccer fields), tangentially related to Target 11.7 on access to safe, inclusive green spaces.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to sustainable consumption, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to climate change mitigation, adaptation, or awareness.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or aquatic resources.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.
